default: (a) {@item chain shirt|phb} and a {@item shield|phb} or (b) {@item leather armor|phb}
(a) two {@item shortsword|phb|shortswords} or (b) two {@filter simple melee weapons|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=simple weapon;melee weapon=sand}
(a) a {@item longbow|phb} and a {@item quiver|phb} of {@item arrows (20)|phb|20 arrows} or (b) a {@filter martial weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon}
(a) a {@item dungeoneer's pack|phb} or (b) an {@item explorer's pack|phb}

entries.name: The Ranger
entries: As the graying man made his way through the underbrush of the forest his faded green cloak made him nearly invisible. The monstrously large troll he had been stalking through the night had finally stopped to rest. As the great brute lay down to sleep, the silent green shape emerged from the trees to snuff out the life of the hideous monster.

As the massive blue dragon descended upon the small town, a small half-elven man rolled out of the path of a blast of lightning. With the small hairs on the back of his neck still standing up from the residual electricity, the practiced hunter nocked an arrow and let it fly in the direction of the terrible beast. As the dart struck, the dragon let out a horrible roar and plummeted to the ground landing in a broken heap.

As the fearsome owlbear lumbered toward the tiny hamlet that she called home, a small halfling whistled to her companion in the brush. At her signal, a giant dog that seemed to be more fur than anything else leaped out from hiding and stood defiantly in front of the owlbear. Distracted by her partner, the owlbear was caught completely unawares by the diminutive ranger. Once their foe was unconscious, the halfling and her dog released the owlbear miles away where it could live without menacing the town they protected.
All three of the adventurers described here are considered Rangers, wild wanderers, and defenders of the natural world.

entries.name: Defenders of Balance
entries: Rangers spend their lives between two worlds. On one hand, they stand as guardians of civilization, protecting those who dwell on the edges of society. On the other, rangers are expert survivalists, deeply acquainted with the flora and fauna of the natural world. Rangers protect the balance between society and the wilderness. When a wild creature wanders from its natural habitat, Rangers will track it down before it can harm innocent farmers and travelers. When people encroach upon the wilderness, Rangers will push back against industry and expansion that doesn't respect the natural balance.
Rangers dedicate their lives to guarding the places where civilization and the wilderness meet, never fully joining either of the worlds that they spend their lives defending.

entries.name: Prepared for Anything
entries: Ready to face any challenge, Rangers are survivalists at their core. The wilderness is a harsh place, and those who cannot adapt to their surroundings die. In the wild, Rangers learn to be adaptable. Drawing on their knowledge of the natural world, their connection to primal magic, and the skills passed down to them by their mentors, there is rarely a challenge a Ranger cannot overcome with enough time to prepare.

Each Ranger has their own philosophy on how they fit into the interconnected web that is the natural world. Some view themselves as apex predators, using their marital skills and ruthless hunting abilities to keep the wild in line. Others see themselves as humble servants of the natural world.

entries.name: Creating Your Ranger
entries: Consider the nature of your Ranger's training. Did you train with a mentor, wandering the wilds together, learning all you could? Or did you forge your primal connection to the natural world on your own? What is it that motivates your Ranger? Do you have a vendetta against a certain type of monster? Are you a warrior of the wild who feels more comfortable in the silence of the forest than in city streets or sleepy towns?

No matter their origin, almost all Rangers take up a life of adventuring to impart their knowledge of the wild to the next generation. Often, in their later years, these wild warriors will take on an apprentice, passing on all their knowledge so that the safety of the wilderness is secured for years to come.

entries.name: Quick Build
entries: You can make a Ranger quickly by using these suggestions: First, make Dexterity your highest ability score, followed by your Wisdom. Second, choose the {@background Outlander} background.

subclass.name: Swarm Keeper
subclassFeatures: One with the Swarm|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Swarm Keeper|LLAR|15

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 1st level, you learn one Fighting Style of your choice from the list at the end of this class description. To learn a Fighting Style, you must meet any prerequisites it has, and you cannot learn the same Fighting Style more than once.
When you gain a level, you can replace this Fighting Style with another Fighting Style available to the Ranger class.

classFeature.name: Knacks
entries: In the wild, you have learned bits of primal knowledge, known as {@filter Knacks|optionalfeatures|feature type=LL:SK}, which bolster your hunting, survival, and tracking skills. At 1st level, you know one Knack of your choice from the list at the end of this class.

Whenever you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one Knack you know with a Knack of your choice, so long as you meet the prerequisites for that Knack. You cannot replace a Knack that is a prerequisite for another Knack you know.
At certain Ranger levels, you learn additional Knacks, as shown in the Knacks Known column of the Ranger table.

classFeature.name: Ranger's Quarry
entries: You can hone in on a foe like the great wild predators. At 2nd level, you can use your bonus action to mark one creature you can see as your Quarry, gaining certain benefits against it:

items: Any time you hit your Quarry with an attack, you deal bonus damage equal to your Quarry Die, which is a {@dice d4}.
If you make an ability check to track, analyze, or recall knowledge about it, add your Quarry Die to your roll.

entries: The creature remains your Quarry for 1 hour. The benefits end early if the Quarry is slain, or you mark another creature. If a feature uses your Quarry Die, always roll the Quarry Die.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), then you must expend a spell slot to use it again. You regain one use each time you finish a short rest, and all uses when you finish a long rest.
At certain levels, the size of your Quarry Die increases, as shown in the Quarry Die column of the Ranger class table.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: At 2nd level, you learn to draw upon the primal magic of the natural world to enhance your survival and combat skills:

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: During a long rest, you must spend 1 hour preparing the list of Ranger Spells that are available for you to cast. Choose a number of spells equal to your Wisdom modifier + half your Ranger level, rounded down. To prepare a spell, it must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

The Ranger class table shows the number and level of the spell slots you have to cast your spells. To cast a Ranger spell of 1st-level or higher, you expend a slot of that spell's level or higher. You regain all spell slots when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Your power is drawn from your bond with nature, so Wisdom is your spellcasting ability. Use Wisdom when a spell refers to Spellcasting ability, your Spell save DC, or a spell attack roll:

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use a {@item druidic focus|phb} as the spellcasting focus for your Ranger spells. See the Player's Handbook for example foci.

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast any Ranger spell you have prepared as a Ritual, so long as that spell has the Ritual tag.

classFeature.name: Ranger Conclave
entries: At 3rd level, you choose an Conclave from the list below that best represents the training and skills of your Ranger: Beast Master, Hunter, Spellbreaker, or Monster Slayer.
Your Conclave grants features at 3rd, 7th, 11th, and 15th level. All Conclave features use your Ranger Spell save DC.

Additionally, each Ranger Conclave has a list of Conclave Spells that you always have prepared once you reach the Ranger levels noted in your Conclave's description. Your Conclave Spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they do not count against your total number of spells that you can prepare each day.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1. As normal, you can't increase one of your ability scores above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. Moreover, if you use your action to cast a Ranger spell, you can make a single weapon attack with your bonus action on that same turn.

classFeature.name: Feral Senses
entries: You hunt with the skills of an apex predator. At 5th level, you can't have disadvantage on attack rolls against your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry}.
When you reach 18th level, you cannot have disadvantage on attack rolls against any target within 30 feet of you.

classFeature.name: Conclave Feature
entries: At 7th level, you gain a feature granted by your Ranger Conclave.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1. As normal, you can't increase one of your ability scores above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Tireless
entries: You are always prepared to hunt, never letting foes rest. Beginning at 10th level, you regain all uses of Ranger's Quarry whenever you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Conclave Feature
entries: At 11th level, you gain a feature granted by your Ranger Conclave.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1. As normal, you can't increase one of your ability scores above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Conclave Feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Ranger Conclave.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1. As normal, you can't increase one of your ability scores above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1. As normal, you can't increase one of your ability scores above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Foe Slayer
entries: At 20th level, your skill rivals the great hunters of legend. When you hit your Quarry with a weapon attack, you can end your mark to cause it to deal the maximum possible damage, instead of rolling.
Should this special attack reduce your Quarry to 50 hit points or fewer, it must make a Constitution saving throw or be instantly reduced to 0 hit points.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit your Quarry with a weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Beast Master
entries: In their role as guardians of nature, some Rangers form deep connections with the beasts of the wild. In recognition of this relationship, great spirits of the wild known as Primal Beasts will seek out such Rangers and form a primal bond. Together, Master and Beast stand together to defend the natural world.

subclassFeature.name: Beast Master Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Beast
entries: You have entered a mystical bond with a Primal Beast. It is Friendly to you, and uses the following rules:

entries.name: Statistics
entries: Choose a stat block on the next page that best reflects the nature of the Primal Beast: {@creature Beast of the Cave|LLAR|Beast of the Cave}, {@creature Beast of the Land|LLAR|Land}, {@creature Beast of the Sea|LLAR|Sea}, or {@creature Beast of the Sky|LLAR|Sky}. It uses your Proficiency Bonus (PB), Quarry Die, and Spell save DC for saving throws.

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, your Beast acts during your turn. As a bonus action, you can order it to take an action in its stat block, or another action. If you take the Attack action, you can forgo one attack to order your Beast to attack instead. The Beast can move and use its reaction on its own, but will only take the Dodge action unless you give it an order.
If you are Incapacitated, the Beast acts on its own, and will defend you and itself to the best of its ability.

entries.name: Primal Shift
entries: During a long rest, you can perform a 1 hour ritual to change the Primal Beast's stat block to another Primal Beast.

entries.name: Death
entries: If your Beast is reduced to 0 hit points, it must make Death Saving Throws like a player character would. If it dies, you can perform a 1-hour ritual, which can be during a short or long rest, to restore it to life with 1 hit point. As part of this ritual, it can spend Hit Dice to regain additional hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Empathy
entries: Your Primal Beast marks you as a friend to all animals. You gain proficiency in Insight and learn {@optfeature Wild Insight I|LLAR}. It can't be replaced, but it doesn't count against your Knacks Known.
If you already know {@optfeature Wild Insight I|LLAR}, you learn another Knack of your choice that you meet the prerequisites for.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Focus
entries: Ranger and Beast hunt in deadly tandem. Your Primal Beast gains all the benefits of your Ranger's Quarry, including any Knacks you know that enhance it.
Your Primal Beast's Natural Weapon attacks also count as magical for overcoming Resistances and Immunities.

subclassFeature.name: Exceptional Training
entries: You are able to draw out the full ferocity of your Primal Beast. Once per turn when you command your {@subclassFeature Primal Beast|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Beast Master|LLAR|3} to use one of the attacks from its stat block, it can make two attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Bond
entries: The bond with your Primal Beast has reached its apex. When you cast a Ranger spell that targets yourself, the {@subclassFeature Primal Beast|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Beast Master|LLAR|3} also gains the benefits as long as it is within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Deep Stalker
entries: Deep beneath the earth where the light of the sun is a dream, nameless things dwell in the deep shadow. The Rangers who stand against these unspeakable terrors of the darkness are known as Deep Stalkers. Though their skills were originally developed by denizens of the Underdark, Deep Stalkers can be found guarding anywhere darkness lies heavy on the land.

subclassFeature.name: Deep Stalker Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Dread Ambusher
entries: You mimic the surprise tactics of the nameless creatures that dwell in the darkness. You gain a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1), so long as you are not {@quickref surprise||3||surprised} or {@condition incapacitated}.
Also, when you take the {@action Attack} action during your first turn in combat, you can make one additional attack as part of that action. On hit, it deals bonus damage equal to your Quarry Die.

activation.condition: When you take the Attack action during your first turn in combat

subclassFeature.name: Umbral Sight
entries: You have adapted to stalk your prey without light. You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you have Darkvision from another feature, its range increases by 60 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Cloak of Shadows
entries: You have learned to conceal yourself even from the creatures native to the deep shadow. While you are in darkness, you are {@condition invisible} to creatures that rely on {@sense darkvision} to see you.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Mind
entries: You have trained your mind to resist the mental assaults that are common in the depths you stalk. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws. If you are already proficient, you gain proficiency in either Intelligence or Charisma saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Precise Strikes
entries: In the unforgiving dark, every strike could be the difference between life and death. Once per turn when you miss with a weapon attack against your Quarry, you add your Quarry Die to your attack roll, possibly turning your miss into a hit.
Your Darkvision has also improved, allowing you to see in both magical and nonmagical dim light and darkness.

activation.condition: Once per turn when you miss your Quarry with a weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Counter
entries: You can manipulate the shadows to distract your foes. When you see your Quarry target you with an attack, you can use your reaction to cloak yourself in shadows, and impose disadvantage on its attack roll.

If its attack to misses, you can make one weapon attack against your Quarry as part of the same reaction. If you are in dim light or darkness when you make this attack, you have advantage on your attack roll.

activation.condition: When the Quarry you can see targets you with an attack roll

subclassFeature.name: Drake Guard
entries: Sometimes wandering Rangers enter into the service of great dragons and are gifted a portion of their power. Undergoing an ancient ritual, they are bound to a Draconic Companion, a powerful scion of an elder dragon. Known as Drake Guards, these Rangers fight alongside their Companions as powerful draconic servants, and can eventually become dragon riders.

entries.name: Sword, Sorcery, and Dragons!
entries: Interested in playing a true dragon rider? Check out the {@class Magus|LLMC|Order of Scales|Dragon Knight|LLMC} for the {@loader Magus Class|class/LaserLlama; Magus Class.json}, the arcane half-caster companion to the Paladin and Ranger!

subclassFeature.name: Wyrmsoul
entries: The magic that bonds you to your Companion has infused you with draconic power. You learn to speak, read, and write Draconic, the language of dragons.

Also, you can cause your Ranger's Quarry damage, or any damage you deal with a Ranger spell, to be your {@creature Draconic Companion|LLAR|Companion}'s Essence damage in place of its normal damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Draconic Companion
entries: Your soul has been bound to a powerful {@creature Draconic Companion|LLAR|Draconic Companion} through an ancient draconic rite. It uses the following rules:

entries.name: Draconic Essence
entries: Choose either acid, cold, fire, lightning, or poison to be the Essence of your Companion. Once chosen it cannot be changed short of an ancient dragon's magic. This Essence determines many of its features.

entries.name: Statistics
entries: Your Draconic Companion is Friendly to your allies, and fervently loyal to you. It uses the stat block on this page. It uses your Proficiency Bonus (PB), Quarry Die, and Ranger Spell save DC for any saving throws.
Despite its size, your Draconic Companion is too proud to bear another creature as a rider.

entries.name: Combat
entries: In combat, the Companion acts during your turn. It can move and use its reaction independently, but it will only take the Dodge action unless you give it an order. As a bonus action, you can order it to take an action from its stat block or a different action. Moreover, when you take the Attack action, you can forgo one of your attacks and order your Companion to make a single attack instead.
If you are Incapacitated, your Companion can act on its own, and will defend you and itself to the best of its abilities.

entries.name: Death
entries: If your Companion is reduced to 0 hit points, it makes Death Saving Throws like a player character would.

Should your Draconic Companion die, you can perform a 1-hour ritual, which can be during a short or long rest, which restores it to life with 1 hit point. As part of this ritual, it can spend its Hit Dice to regain additional hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Drake Guard Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Companion
entries: Your bond infuses your Companion with greater power. Your Companion becomes Medium in size, and will bear you (and no other) as a rider if you are Medium or smaller. However, if you are riding it, your Companion's flying speed is halved.
Your Companion's Natural Weapon attacks also count as magical for overcoming Resistances and Immunities.

subclassFeature.name: Elemental Breath
entries: As an action you, or your {@creature Draconic Companion|LLAR|Companion}, can exhale a 30-foot cone of Essence energy, forcing creatures in the area to make a Dexterity saving throw. They take Essence damage equal to six Quarry Dice on a failure, and half that on a success.

You and your Companion can use this feature a combined number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. When you have no uses left, you can expend a spell slot of 3rd-level or higher to use your Elemental Breath again.

subclassFeature.name: Mythic Companion
entries: Your bond with your Companion has unlocked its true power. Your Companion now uses the {@creature Mythic Companion|LLAR|Mythic Companion} stat block.
Also, once per turn when you command your Companion to attack, it can make two attacks instead of one.

subclassFeature.name: Fey Wanderer
entries: In your wild wanderings you have acquired the magic of the Feywild, and use it to augment your Ranger abilities. Whether you are the chosen warden of a Fey crossroads, the favored servant of an Archfey, or you pilfered your power in a fey wager, you now wield the whimsical and terrifying powers common to the Feywild.

subclassFeature.name: Fey Wanderer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Beguiling Strikes
entries: You weave the wild and disorienting faerie magic into your strikes. Your Ranger's Quarry bonus damage is psychic, and when you deal this psychic damage to a Quarry that is within 15 feet of you, it has disadvantage on all attack rolls against creatures other than you until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Otherworldly Mystique
entries: You are infused with the magical charm of the faerie. You gain proficiency in Deception, Performance, or Persuasion.
Also, whenever you would make a Charisma check, you can use Wisdom instead.

subclassFeature.name: Whimsical Ward
entries: Your familiarity with faerie magick makes you resistant to its power. You have advantage on saving throws to resist and end the Charmed and Frightened conditions.

Also, if a creature succeeds on its saving throw against one of your Fey Wanderer spells, you can cause another creature of your choice within range to become the target of that spell. You can only do so once per Fey Wanderer spell you cast.
You can choose a new target for a spell a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of 1). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

activation.condition: If a creature succeeds on its saving throw against one of your Fey Wanderer spells

subclassFeature.name: Faerie Guardian
entries: You can call support from the otherworldly faerie courts. You can cast {@spell conjure fey (alternate)|llspell} without its material component.

Moreover, when you cast {@spell conjure fey (alternate)|llspell}, you can cast it so that it doesn't require concentration, but its duration is reduced to 1 minute. If you cast it again, any previous Fey are dispelled.

activation.condition: When you cast conjure fey

subclassFeature.name: Mirthful Step
entries: You can use your magic to slip through the faerie realm. You can cast the {@spell misty step} spell without expending a spell slot a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all uses when you finish a long rest.

Moreover, any time you cast {@spell misty step}, you can touch one willing creature within 5 feet and teleport it along with you. It reappears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the space that you appear in.

subclassFeature.name: Hunter
entries: Hunters hold the line between civilization and the wilderness using their deadly tracking and hunting skills. In the shadows of every countryside town lurks a Hunter, silently taking care of any monster that threatens the peace. Hordes of orcs, vile trolls, great and terrible dragons, or great beasts of the wild, it matters not. A Hunter will always overcome their quarry.

subclassFeature.name: Ambuscade
entries: You are always prepared to strike first, often getting the jump on your chosen prey. When you roll initiative, so long as you are not {@quickref surprise||3||surprised}, you can use your reaction to take the {@action Hide} action, the {@action Attack} action, or mark a creature as your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry}.
If you are Surprised, you cannot take this special reaction, but you can act normally on your first turn of combat.

activation.condition: When you roll initiative, so long as you are not surprised

subclassFeature.name: Hunter Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

rows: {@spell mordenkainen's faithful hound||faithful hound}, {@spell freedom of movement}

subclassFeature.name: Silent Steps
entries: You stalk your prey unseen until it is too late. You gain proficiency in Stealth and you learn the {@optfeature Stalker I|LLAR} Knack. It cannot be replaced, but does not count against your Knacks Known.
If you already know Stalker I, you can learn a different Knack of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Cunning Parry
entries: Your unyielding focus allows you to parry and counter foe's attacks. When your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} targets you with an attack you can see, you can use your reaction to add your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1) to your Armor Class against that attack.
If this causes the attack to miss, you can make one weapon attack against your Quarry as part of that same reaction.

activation.condition: When your Quarry targets you with an attack you can see

subclassFeature.name: Relentless Hunter
entries: You are relentless, stalking your chosen foes until they are all eradicated. When your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} is slain, you can mark another creature you can see as your Quarry (no action required) without expending a use of Ranger's Quarry or a spell slot.

activation.condition: When your Quarry is slain

subclassFeature.name: Horde Breaker
entries: You are able to cut your chosen prey down as blades of grass. You gain the features listed below, each of which can be used as an action on each of your turns. You must be wielding the correct weapon in order to use the corresponding feature:

subclassFeature.name: Volley
entries: Choose a point you can see in the normal Range of your ranged weapon. You fire a volley of ammunition, forcing creatures of your choice within 10 feet of that point to make a Dexterity saving throw. Creatures take damage as if they had been hit by an attack from your ranged weapon on a failure, and half as much damage on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Whirlwind
entries: You strike at the enemies around you, forcing creatures of your choice within range of your melee weapon to make a Dexterity saving throw. Creatures take damage as if they had been hit by your melee weapon on a failure, and half as much damage on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Ruthless Focus
entries: Your skills as a hunter rival those of legends and myths. When you damage to your Quarry with an attack or Horde Breaker, you can add your Wisdom modifier (a minimium of +1) to the damage.

subclassFeature.name: Monster Slayer
entries: Some Rangers are driven from civilized life by a deep hatred for monsters. Often victims of these terrible monsters in their youth, those that take up the mantle of Monster Slayer travel the world hunting for vile supernatural creatures that prey on innocent mortals and ruthlessly annihilate them. They track down vampires, evil fey, restless spirits, werewolves, and any other vile creature and destroy them without hesitation.

entries.subclassFeature: Knowledge of the Hunt|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Monster Slayer|LLAR|3

subclassFeature.name: Knowledge of the Hunt
entries: You have dedicated yourself to the destruction of creatures of darkness, and study their weaknesses. You gain proficiency in your choice of {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}.

As a bonus action, you can analyze a creature you can see within 30 feet, making an ability check using the skill below corresponding to its creature type (DC equals 8 + its CR). If the target is your Quarry, add your Quarry Die to your roll:

On a successful check, you learn one of the following characteristics of your choice: its highest ability score, lowest ability score, its damage vulnerabilities, its damage resistances or immunities, its condition immunities, or another weakness it may have. If the characteristic you choose doesn't apply the DM reveals another characteristic to you.
Alternately, you can choose to learn its highest ability score, lowest ability score, Armor Class, a Trait in its stat block, or another weakness it may have as determined by the DM.
If you fail your ability check, you can't use this feature on that creature again until you hit it with an attack.

subclassFeature.name: Monster Slayer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

rows: {@spell bane}, {@spell protection from evil and good||protection from evil & good}
{@spell dispel evil and good||dispel evil & good}, {@spell hold monster}

subclassFeature.name: Hated Foe
entries: Your skills have been honed for a particular hated foe. You learn the {@optfeature Favored Foe|LLAR} Knack.
Once in between each short or long rest, you can mark one of your Favored Foes as your Quarry without spending a use of the feature or a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Iron Focus
entries: You have trained your mind to better resist the supernatural assaults of your hated foes. Whenever your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} forces you to make an ability check or saving throw you gain a bonus to your roll equal to one roll of your Quarry Die.

subclassFeature.name: Monster's Nemesis
entries: Your hatred for evil empowers your weapons. Your weapon attacks against your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} treat its damage immunities as resistances, and ignore any damage resistances it has.
Moreover, when your Quarry misses you with an attack or you succeed on a saving throw it forced you to make, you can use your reaction to make a single weapon attack against it.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: When your Quarry misses you with an attack or you succeed on a saving throw it forced you to make

subclassFeature.name: Ruthless Counter
entries: You do everything in your power to prevent the monsters you hunt from harming the innocent. When you see your Quarry attempt to cast a spell, teleport, or use another supernatural or magical ability from its stat block, you can make a single weapon attack against it as a reaction. On hit, your Quarry must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or that ability is thwarted and fails to take effect.
When you use this feature and your attack hits, you can instantly end the effects of Ranger's Quarry and cause the Quarry to automatically fail this saving throw.
Should this cause an ability to fail, that use of the ability is still expended by your Quarry.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: When you see your Quarry attempt to cast a spell, teleport, or use another supernatural or magical ability from its stat block

subclassFeature.name: Planar Warden
entries: Planar Wardens dedicate their lives to preserving the balance of the multiverse by defending their native plane of existence from otherworldly threats. Keeping watch over planar portals these esoteric warriors can draw on the power of the planes to destroy outsiders that would seek to invade their homes.

subclassFeature.name: Planar Warden Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

rows: {@spell create snare (alternate)|llspell}, {@spell protection from evil and good||protection from evil & good}

subclassFeature.name: Extraplanar Awareness
entries: You have trained to sense otherworldly presences which may threaten your home. As an action, you open your senses, and learn the distance and direction of the closest creature not native to the current plane, or the nearest planar portal within 1 mile of your location. As part of this action, you can exclude any creature or portal you are aware of. If a creature or portal is shielded from Divination magic, you do not detect it

If you detect something, you can mark it as your Quarry without expending a use, and for the duration, you know the direction and distance to it, so long as it remains on the same plane of existence as you.
You can use this special sense once per short or long rest at no cost, after that, you must expend a spell slot to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Planar Warrior
entries: You can augment your strikes with power drawn from the space between the planes. Your Ranger's Quarry bonus damage is force.
Moreover, if your Quarry is a creature that is not native to your current plane of existence, the bonus damage from Ranger's Quarry is doubled to two Quarry Dice.

subclassFeature.name: Ethereal Step
entries: You can briefly move between planes to disorient your foes. When you take the Attack action, you can teleport up to 10 feet before each attack to an unoccupied space you can see.
Any distance you teleport with this feature counts against your total movement for your turn.

activation.condition: When you take the Attack action

subclassFeature.name: Elite Warden
entries: You are an elite channeler of planar magic. Whenever you use Ethereal Step before a weapon attack, you can choose for all damage from that attack to become force damage.

Moreover, if you use Ethereal Step and attack at least two different targets, you can make one additional attack as part of that same Attack action. However, this bonus attack must be against a third target.

subclassFeature.name: Extraplanar Adept
entries: Your ability to sense otherworldly incursions is second nature to you. You can use Extraplanar Awareness as a special ritual which takes 10 minutes to complete, and can be done as part of a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ethereal Defense
entries: You can partially phase into the space in between planes to resist hostile strikes. When you take damage from a source you can see, you can use your reaction to gain Resistance to that instance of damage.

When you use this reaction you can expend a spell slot to instantly teleport a number of feet equal to 10 times the level of the spell slot spent, appearing in an unoccupied space you can see within that range.

activation.condition: When you take damage from a source you can see

subclassFeature.name: Mage Breaker
entries: You ward your weapons with antimagic enchantments. When you hit your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} with a weapon attack, it has disadvantage on its subsequent Constitution saving throw to maintain {@condition concentration} on spells.
Also, whenever you see or hear a creature within your reach attempt to cast a spell, you can use your reaction to make an opportunity attack against it.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: Whenever you see or hear a creature within your reach attempt to cast a spell

subclassFeature.name: Spellbreaker
entries: Spellbreakers are a small but dedicated Conclave of Rangers that specialize in hunting mages who use their arcane power for evil. While most pursue the life of a Spellbreaker for noble reasons, there are some who seek to destroy anyone with the potential to use magic. Most Spellbreakers work in secrecy, only striking when success is a certainty. If a wrong move could end in disintegration, there is no room for error.

entries.name: Arcane Spellbreaker
entries: Looking for a more dedicated mage hunter? Check out the {@class Magus|LLMC|Order of Spellbreakers|Spellbreaker|LLMC} for the {@loader Magus Class|class/LaserLlama; Magus Class.json}.
The Magus is an Intelligence-based, arcane half-caster that serves as the counterpart to the Ranger and Paladin. Hunt with mastery of spell & sword!

subclassFeature.name: Spellbreaker Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. They count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare.

rows: {@spell arcane eye}, {@spell otiluke's resilient sphere||resilient sphere}
{@spell dispel evil and good||dispel evil & good}, {@spell wall of force (alternate)|llspell}

subclassFeature.name: Spellsight
entries: You have learned to see the arcane potential of creatures. When you mark a creature as your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry}, you instantly learn its spellcasting ability (if it has one) and the level of the highest level spell that it has the ability to cast.
If your Quarry is hidden from divination magic, such as by a {@spell nondetection} spell, it appears as if it cannot cast spells.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Defense
entries: You can steel yourself to better resist the magics of your foes. Whenever your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} forces you to make a saving throw to resist a spell or another magical effect, you gain a bonus to your roll equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

Moreover, whenever your Quarry subjects you to a spell or magical effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage on a success, and only half damage on a failed saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Spellbane
entries: You are an unparalleled hunter of mages. Whenever you hit your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} with a weapon attack, you can expend a spell slot of 1st-level or higher to deal bonus force damage equal to one roll of your Quarry Die per level of the spell slot.
Also, whenever you cast {@spell counterspell (alternate)|llspell} or {@spell dispel magic}, you can add a roll of your Quarry Die to the ability check.

activation.condition: Whenever you hit your Quarry with a weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Mantle of the Master
entries: Your Spellbreaker training has reached its pinnacle. You are resistant to damage from spells and all other magical effects so long as you are not {@condition unconscious} or {@condition incapacitated}.

subclassFeature.name: Reflect Spell
entries: Your training allows you to strike spells from the weave. You can cast {@spell absorb elements|xge} at will as if you cast it at 1st-level.
After you cast absorb elements, if your next melee weapon attack is against the creature whose spell you absorbed, you treat the absorb elements damage as its maximum amount.

subclassFeature.name: Biting Assault
entries: One creature of your choice within 30 feet must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take piercing or poison damage equal to two rolls of your Quarry Die.

subclassFeature.name: Harrying Horde
entries: One creature of your choice within 30 feet must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be moved up to 15 feet in a direction of your choice. Large and larger creatures have advantage on this Strength saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Keeper
entries: Swarm Keepers enter symbiotic relationships with swarms of tiny creatures. Most often surrounded by a swarm of rodents, insects, birds, or other small animals, these strange Rangers see themselves as guardians of the smallest lives. Dwelling in the deep woods or underground caves, Swarm Keepers build communities that work together for the good of the swarm.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Keeper Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Step
entries: You are moved up to 15 feet in a direction of your choice without provoking opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Symbiotic Swarm
entries: You have become an integral part of a swarm of vermin. You know the {@spell mage hand} cantrip, but when you cast mage hand, it manifests as part of your swarm which does your bidding.
Also, you can use a bonus action on each of your turns to command your swarm in one of the following ways:

subclassFeature.name: Writhing Horde
entries: You can compel your swarm to carry you in unnatural ways. You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb sheer surfaces and upside down on ceilings without having to make an ability check. While climbing in this way, you are surrounded by your swarm of vermin.

Also, when you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check to {@action shove} or {@action grapple}, or to resist or end a {@condition grappled||grapple}, you can call on your swarm and add one roll of your Quarry Die to your roll.

activation.condition: Also, when you make a Strength (Athletics) check to shove or grapple, or to resist or end a grapple

subclassFeature.name: Greater Swarm
entries: Your swarm has grown vastly in both numbers and in power. Your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} has disadvantage on saving throws against your {@subclassFeature Symbiotic Swarm|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Swarm Keeper|LLAR|3} features. Your Symbiotic Swarm features also gain the following additional benefits:

entries.name: Biting Assault
entries: On a failed save, the creature is {@condition poisoned} until the start of your next turn in addition to the damage.

entries.name: Harrying Horde
entries: On a failed save, the creature is knocked {@condition prone} at the end of this forced movement.

entries.name: Swarm Step
entries: After you move, you also gain the benefits of {@quickref cover||3||half cover} until the beginning of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: One with the Swarm
entries: You can fade into your swarm to avoid danger. When you take damage from a source you can see, you can use your reaction to become one with your swarm and give yourself resistance to that instance of damage. You and your swarm then teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet.

You can use this special reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. If you have no uses left, you can expend a spell slot of 1st-level or higher to use this special reaction again.

activation.condition: When you take damage from a source you can see

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Master
entries: You have become one with your swarm, and can command it with only your mind. Once per long rest, you can cast one of your Swarm Keeper Spells without expending a spell slot or requiring its normal material components.

subclassFeature.name: Bounty Hunter
entries: Bounty Hunters protect humanoids from their own kind. Trained to stalk the streets of cities, they track their prey through the dark alleyways and underbellies of any town. Trained to use complex combat techniques, these Rangers can confidently confront and subdue any humanoid who threatens peace.

entries.subclassFeature: Ear to the Ground|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Bounty Hunter|LLAR:E|3

subclassFeature.name: Ear to the Ground
entries: Your work gives you insight into the criminal underworld. You gain proficiency in Investigation, and you learn the {@optfeature Urban Adept|LLAR} Knack. It does not count against your Knacks Known, but it cannot be replaced. If you already know it, you gain another Knack.

subclassFeature.name: Martial Exploits
entries: You have studied various martial exploits to better counter the humanoids you hunt. You gain the following features:

entries.name: High Degree
entries: Your Ranger level limits the technicality of Exploits you are able to learn an use. This limit is reflected in the High Degree column of the Bounty Hunter Exploits table.

entries.name: Exploits
entries: You learn two {@filter Martial Exploits|optionalfeatures|feature type=ll:me} of your choice from those available to the {@class Alternate Fighter|LLAF}. You can only use one Exploit per ability check, attack, or saving throw.
When you gain a Ranger level, you can replace one Exploit you know with another Martial Exploit of your choice.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If an Exploit requires a creature to make a saving throw, the saving throw DC is calculated as follows:

entries.name: Multiclassing & Exploits
entries: Your martial skill depends partly on your combined levels in classes with Exploits, and partly on levels in each class. If you learn Exploits from more than one class, reference {@link Alternate Martial Multiclassing|https://www.gmbinder.com/share/-NGUL51kfZCPlESxL1wq}.

subclassFeature.name: Exploit Dice
entries: The Bounty Hunter Exploits table shows how many Exploit Dice you have to use Exploits. To use an Exploit, you expend an Exploit Die, and you regain all your expended Exploit Dice when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Cunning Restraint
entries: When you make an Athletics check to Grapple or Shove your Quarry, you add your Quarry Die to your roll. Moreover, when your Quarry is Grappled by you or Prone, it has disadvantage on saving throws to resist your Exploits and Ranger spells.

activation.condition: Once per turn, when you force your Quarry to make a saving throw to resist the effects of an Exploit

subclassFeature.name: Ruthless Counter
entries: You masterfully disable your foes. When your Quarry misses you with a melee attack, you can force it to make a Dexterity saving throw as a reaction. On a failure, you knock it Prone, Grapple it, or make one melee attack against it.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: When your Quarry misses you with a melee attack

subclassFeature.name: Most Dangerous Game
entries: Supernatural instinct allow you to predict your foe's attacks. Once per turn when you take damage from your Quarry, you reduce the damage you would take by your Quarry Die.

activation.condition: Once per turn when you take damage from your Quarry

subclassFeature.name: Buccaneer
entries: Combining their survival skills, affinity for nature magic, and passion for the high seas, Buccaneers are Rangers who feel more at home on the water than on solid ground. Serving as sailors, pirates, marines, and privateers, they are often among the most valuable crew members on their vessel. What drove you to the sea? Were you pressed into service by a pirate king? Or, have you sworn to slay a sea monster?

subclassFeature.name: Buccaneer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Brazen Bravado
entries: You walk with the confidence that can only come from many near-death experiences. So long as you are not Incapacitated or Surprised, you gain a bonus to your iniatitive rolls and saving throws to resist and end the Frightened condition equal to your Quarry Die.

subclassFeature.name: Sea Legs
entries: Your time on the sea has granted you a specific set of surival skills. You gain either the {@optfeature Mariner|LLStyles} Fighting Style or {@optfeature Aquatic Adept|LLAR} Knack. Your choice does not count against your total Fighting Styles or Knacks Known, but cannot be replaced.
If you already know both {@optfeature Aquatic Adept|LLAR} and the {@optfeature Mariner|LLStyles} Fighting Style, you gain the Druidic Warrior Fighting Style.

subclassFeature.name: First Mate
entries: You are one of the most valuable crew members aboard your vessel. You add your Quarry Die to any ability check related to the navigation or operation of a seafaring vessel.

Moreover, when you are captaining a seafaring vessel, you can mark another vessel or a Huge or larger creature with a swimming speed as your Quarry, and every Friendly creature aboard your vessel gains the benefits of Ranger's Quarry so long as you remain abord the vessel as captain.

subclassFeature.name: Brine in the Blood
entries: You have gained an affinity for the tempestuous magic of the seas. When you finish a short or long rest, you align yourself with one aspect of the seas, gaining the benefits below:

entries.name: Waves
entries: You gain Resistance to cold damage, your bonus damage from Ranger's Quarry becomes cold damage, and you cannot be knocked Prone or moved against your will.

entries.name: Winds
entries: You gain Resistance to thunder damage, your bonus damage from Ranger's Quarry is thunder damage, and you ignore difficult terrain while walking and swimming.

subclassFeature.name: Wrath of the Sea
entries: You carry the magic of the high seas whether you are on sea or land. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you imbue the strike with tempestuous magic, forcing it to make a Strength saving throw. If the target is your Quary, it has disadvantage. On a failed save, it suffers the effect that corresponds your Brine in the Blood alignment.

entries.name: Waves
entries: The creature takes bonus cold damage equal to your Quarry Die and it is knocked back a number of feet in a line equal to 5 times your Wisdom modifier (minimum 5 feet), but this distance is halved if the creature is larger than you.

entries.name: Winds
entries: The creature takes bonus thunder damage equal to your Quarry Die, and after this attack you can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

activation.condition: Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Scion of the Seas
entries: You are a child of the great waters. You gain the benefits of both Brine in the Blood alignments, but you only choose one when you use Wrath of the Sea.
Finally, when you are aboard a Friendly seafaring vessel, its speed is doubled and you can use your reaction to reduce any damage it takes by your Ranger level.

subclassFeature.name: Grim Warden
entries: Sometimes it takes a monster to destroy a monster. Those who take up the mantle of Grim Warden undergo a sinister alchemical ritual, the Warden's Rite, which enhances their physical abilities. They sacrifice any chance they have at a normal life by mingling their blood with that of monsters, so they may gain unnatural power to destroy their foes.

entries.name: A Monster to Fight Monsters
entries: If you like the idea of a full class that uses sinister blood magic, check out the {@loader Alternate Blood Hunter|class/LaserLlama; Alternate Blood Hunter.json}

subclassFeature.name: Crimson Mark
entries: When you mark a creature as a Quarry, you can make a blood sacrifice in place of spending a use of Ranger's Quarry. When you do, reduce both your current and maximum hit points by your Quarry Die. Any reductions to your maximum hit points from this feature are undone at the end of your next long rest.

For the duration, your Quarry damage is necrotic, and each time you deal this damage, you can gain temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt. When your Quarry takes this necrotic damage it cannot regain hit points for 1 minute.

activation.condition: When you mark a creature as your Quarry

subclassFeature.name: Grim Warden Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at Ranger levels indicated below. They count as Ranger spells for you, but don't count against the spells you prepare.

subclassFeature.name: Warden's Rite
entries: You have studied and undergone the Warden's Rite, an unholy alchemical ritual which enhances your blood. You gain proficiency in Alchemist's Supplies and Religion, and you add your Quarry Die to any ability check you make with either.
Also, you count as a Monstrosity along with your normal creature types, and your maximum hit points increase by 3, and increase by 1 again each time you gain a Ranger level.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Augmentation
entries: Sinister magic in your blood enhances your physical abilities. While you have a Crimson Mark on a creature, your walking speed increases by 10 feet, and you can add your Quarry Die to all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution ability checks.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Strikes
entries: Powerful blood magic infuses your weapons. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can deal an additional 1d10 necrotic damage to it. When you do so, you can choose to gain temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

activation.condition: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Sinister Resilience
entries: The dark magic that flows in your blood grants you defenses against evil magicks. You gain Resistance to necrotic damage, and when you reduce your hit points with Crimson Mark, you roll your Quarry Die twice and use the lower result.
Also, any necrotic damage you deal ignores Resistance, and treats Immunity to necrotic damage as Resistance.

subclassFeature.name: Sanguine Mastery
entries: The magic of your Warden's Rite manifests its full potential. You have advantage on saving throws to resist the Frightened condition, and you are always under the effects of protection from good & evil while you are conscious.
Finally, when you are forced to make a Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution saving throw while you have a Crimson Mark on a creature, you gain a bonus equal to your Quarry Die.

subclassFeature.name: Nomad
entries: Whether by birth, happenstance, or a ritual, the mind of every Nomad has been connected to the mystical web of knowledge known as the Noosphere. This psionic link allows Nomads to access knowledge from far-off places and distant lives. These strange wanderers spend their lives collecting hidden lore to add to this web of information that connects every Nomad.
Drawing on this network, these Rangers can use psionic abilities to slip through cracks in reality, assault the minds of their foes, and draw on the knowledge of other Nomads.

entries.name: Optional Rule: Psionic Spellcasting
entries: For mechanics to match the fantasy of a psionic Ranger, replace all Ranger and Nomad Conclave features that use Wisdom with Intelligence.

subclassFeature.name: Expunging Strikes
entries: You can imbue your strikes with the psionic power of the noosphere to disrupt your foe's mind. Once per turn when you hit your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} with a weapon attack, you can force it to make an Intelligence saving throw against your Ranger Spell save DC. On a failure, all damage dealt by your attack becomes psychic damage, and your Quarry can't see, hear, or sense you in any way until the start of your next turn.

activation.condition: Once per turn when you hit your Quarry with a weapon attack

subclassFeature.name: Mystic Knowledge
entries: You can use your connection to the Noosphere to draw knowledge from the Nomads of past and present. Each time you finish a long rest, you gain proficiency in one skill or tool, or learn to speak, read, and write a language of your choice. You retain the knowledge of this language or skill proficiency until the end of your next long rest.
Beginning at 11th level in this class, you gain two proficiencies or languages of your choice through this feature at the end of a long rest, following these rules.

subclassFeature.name: Nomad Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Nomad's Step
entries: You can temporarily merge your consciousness with the Noosphere to avoid harm and move through the world in strange ways. You gain the two features below. You can use these features a combined number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Phase Defense
entries: When you are hit by an attack you can see, you can use a reaction to disappear into the Noosphere, causing that attack to miss. You then instantly reappear in an unoccupied space that you had occupied at some point since the beginning of your previous turn.

activation.condition: When you are hit by an attack you can see

subclassFeature.name: Psionic Jaunt
entries: As a bonus action, you expend any amount of your remaining movement to instantly teleport a number of feet equal to the movement you expended, reappearing in an unoccupied space you can see within that range.

subclassFeature.name: Strange Movement
entries: Your connection to the Noosphere has grown. You regain all uses of Nomad's Step when you finish a short or long rest.
You also regain one expended use when you roll initiative.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Burst
entries: When reappearing from the Noosphere you can assault your foes with the residual psionic power. When you teleport with a Nomad Spell or Nomad feature, you can force all creatures within 10 feet of the point you appear to make an Intelligence saving throw. They take psychic damage equal to your Quarry Die on a failure, and half as much on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Shifter
entries: Channeling the edler magic of Druids, Rangers of the Shifter Conclave can change their physical form, taking on the shape of the Beasts they defend. They use this elder power to stand as guardians of all wildlife and the natural world.

subclassFeature.name: Limited Wild Shape
entries: As a bonus action, you can channel elder Druidic magick to Shift into {@filter Beast Shapes|bestiary|type=Beast|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts} using the rules below:

entries.name: Beast Shapes
entries: At 3rd level, you instictively know three {@filter Beast Shapes|bestiary|type=Beast|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts} Shapes of CR {@filter 1/4|bestiary|type=Beast|challenge rating=[&0;&1/4]|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts} or lower, all chosen from among living Beasts that you have seen before.

When you Shift, your game statistics are replaced by those of that Beast, but you retain your alignment, hit points, and your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. You also retain your proficiencies, in addition to gaining those of the Beast. If you and a Beast Shape share a proficiency, use the higher bonus.

entries.name: Duration and Uses
entries: You can stay in a Beast Shape for 1 hour, but you revert to your normal form early if you are Incapacitated or if you use a bonus action to do so.
You can use Wild Shape a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain one use after a short rest, and all uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: You can choose for natural equipment, such as objects made of wood, animal parts, or natural materials, to merge with you. Equipment made from highly processed material, like metal weapons or armor, falls to the ground in your space when you Shift into a Beast Shape.

entries.name: Features
entries: You retain all benefits and features from your class, race, or other sources, including Ranger's Quarry, and can still use them so long as your Beast Shape is physically capable of doing so. You do not retain special senses, but use the senses of the Beast. Finally, you can't cast spells, but you can maintain concentration on spells and abilities.

entries.name: Learning New Beast Shapes
entries: You can learn a new Beast Shape by using an action to touch a Friendly Beast of a CR ¼ or lower. There is no limit to your number of Beast Shapes.

subclassFeature.name: Shifter Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Empathy
entries: Elder magic bonds you to Beasts of the wilderness. You gain proficiency in Insight and learn the {@optfeature Wild Insight I|LLAR} Knack. It cannot be replaced, but it doesn't count against your Knacks Known. If you already know Wild Insight I, you learn another Knack of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Elder Power
entries: The elder power you channel increases your abilities. As you unlock new spell slots, as per your Ranger level, you can learn more powerful Beast Shapes, as shown on the table below.
Also, when you Wild Shape, you can expend a spell slot in place of a Wild Shape use to Shift into more powerful Beasts determined by the level of spell slot spent, as shown below:
When you Shift in this way, your Beast Shape's attacks are magical, and you can replace your Beast Shape's damage die with your Quarry Die if your Quarry Die is higher.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Resilience
entries: You are a wild warrior without peer. When you roll initiative, you can either regain one use of Wild Shape, or Wild Shape before any creature has a chance to act.
Moreover, when you Wild Shape you can spend both a spell slot and a use of Wild Shape to have your Beast Shape gain Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Mastery
entries: You have mastered the elder magic of the Druids. Your Wild Shape transformation only ends when you choose to end it.

subclassFeature.name: Stargazer
entries: Since ancient times, mortals have looked to the stars of night for stories of the past, omens for the present, and portents of the future. The Rangers known as Stargazers dedicate their lives to the study of stars and constellations, and can draw on their radiant power to conjure celestial magic, vanquish their foes, and twist the threads of fate for those around them.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Guidance
entries: The constellations of the night sky guide your steps. You gain proficiency in your choice of either {@item cartographer's tools|phb} or {@item navigator's tools|phb}. Also, whenever you make an ability check with either set of tools you add a roll of your Quarry Die to the result.
Finally, you can't become lost, even by magical means so long as you can see the stars of the night sky.

subclassFeature.name: Constellation Magic
entries: You draw your power from the constellations of the night sky. Each time you finish a long rest, you attune yourself to one of the Constellations below. While attuned, you always have the corresponding Constellation spells, one cantrip and 1st-level spell, prepared. They become Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the total number of spells you prepare. They also count as your Conclave Spells for 3rd level.

You can cast your 1st-level Constellation spell once without expending a spell slot, at the level of your highest level slot as shown on the Ranger table for your level, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Stargazer Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Threads of Fate
entries: You can use the insights you gain from the stars to twist fate. When a creature you can see within 30 feet makes an ability check or saving throw, you can use your reaction to add your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1) to the result of its roll. You must use this reaction before you know if it succeeds or fails.
You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all of your expended uses when you finish a long rest.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: When a creature you can see within 30 feet makes an ability check or saving throw

subclassFeature.name: Starlight Strikes
entries: You infuse your weapons with the power of celestial starlight. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack or damage it with a Ranger spell, you deal additional radiant damage equal to one roll of your Quarry Die

activation.condition: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack or damage it with a Ranger spell

subclassFeature.name: Resplendent Soul
entries: When a creature you can see targets you with an attack, you can use a reaction to release a targeted flash of light toward it, forcing it to make a Constitution saving throw against your Spell save DC. On a failure, it takes radiant damage equal to three rolls of your Quarry Die, and the attack misses you.

You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all uses when you finish a long rest. If you have no uses left, you can expend a spell slot of 1st-level or higher to use this again.

activation.condition: When a creature you can see targets you with an attack

subclassFeature.name: Trapper
entries: While all Rangers have some skill with snares and traps, few are true masters of the craft. These Trappers are meticulous planners with mechanical minds who use their skills to plan ambushes and set snares for their foes. They have a talent for anticipating their foe's next move and laying the perfect trap.

subclassFeature.name: Meticulous Fingers
entries: You are exceptionally gifted with mechanical objects and traps. You gain proficiency in {@skill Sleight of Hand} and with {@item Thieves' Tools|phb|Thieves'} and {@item Tinker's Tools|phb}, and whenever you make an ability check with these tools or skill, you add one roll of your Quarry Die to the result.
Moreover, your precise hands allow you to take the {@action Use an Object} action as a bonus action on each of your turns.

subclassFeature.name: Trap Adept
entries: You learn the {@optfeature Trapper I|LLAR} Knack, but it does not count against your total Knacks Known. If you already know the Trapper Knack you can learn another Knack of your choice.

Also, when you have access to the inner workings of a trap that has been disarmed, you can use {@item tinker's tools|phb} to rearm the trap (so long as rearming it is possible), and when you do so, its new DC is equal to your Ranger Spell save DC.

subclassFeature.name: Trapper Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

rows: {@spell stone shape}, {@spell Otiluke's Resilient Sphere||resilient sphere}

subclassFeature.name: Combat Snares
entries: You can quickly assemble and utilize makeshift traps in combat. As an action, you can craft a Trap (using the {@optfeature Trapper I|LLAR} Knack) and throw it at a space you can see within 20 feet. Creatures in the space of the Trap must make a saving throw as if it had moved into the Trap's space, suffering its effects on a failed save as normal. Traps crafted in this way only last for 1 minute.
You can also take the {@action Use an Object} action to throw a Trap that you had already crafted (for example, the Traps crafted with {@optfeature Trapper I|LLAR} Knack) at a creature in this way.

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Snares
entries: A creature marked as your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} has disadvantage on its initial saving throw against Traps you created or modified, and it automatically fails its initial saving throw against your Traps if it is unaware of the Trap's location.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Traps
entries: Your skills as a Trapper have grown. At the end of each long rest, you craft a number of Traps (with {{@optfeature Trapper I|LLAR} Knack)) equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of 2 Traps).

Additionally, any Traps you craft with Trapper now work on creatures that are Huge or smaller, and if a creature makes a saving throw against your Trap, it takes magical bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (your choice) equal to three rolls of your Quarry Die on a failure, and half that on a success.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Traps
entries: You are always ready to lay a trap. When you roll initiative you can use your reaction to craft one Trap and throw it to one unoccupied space you can see within 20 feet of you.
Also, whenever you craft a Trap, you can choose whether it takes up a 10-foot square or a single 5-foot square.

subclassFeature.name: Wastelander
entries: While all Rangers have the skills to survive in the wilds, those known as Wastelanders are true masters of survival. Rugged wanderers, they have adapted to survive in the harshest lands imaginable; arid deserts, frozen tundra, great alpine heights, and magical wastelands. These survivalists combine a talent for illusion magic and protective gear to survive anywhere.

entries.name: Elemental Wastelands
entries: While most Wastelanders hail from the deserts of the material plane, it is not unheard of for them to come from the chaotic inner planes. Some call the elemental planes of air, earth, fire, and water home.

subclassFeature.name: Wasteland Cloak
entries: You have mastered an ancient pattern of enhancing clothing, enabling a supernatural survivability. You gain Proficiency in Weaver's Tools, and add double your Proficiency Bonus to any check you make with these tools.
During a long rest, you can spend 1 hour using Weaver's Tools to create a Wasteland Cloak, which grants the benefits below to any one creature wearing that Cloak:

items: It adds your Quarry Die to Stealth checks made to Hide.
It requires one-quarter as much water in order to survive.
It has advantage on saving throws to resist the effects of harsh environments and subsequent Exhaustion levels.

entries: During each long rests, you can spend 1 hour maintaining all Cloaks you have enhanced or they lose all their beneficial properties. You can maintain a number of Cloaks equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of 1 set of Cloak).

activation.condition: At the end of each long rest

subclassFeature.name: Wastelander Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Wilderness Adept
entries: You are able to survive the most inhospitable environments. You learn one of the following Knacks of your choice: {@optfeature Alpine Adept|LLAR}, {@optfeature Aquatic Adept|LLAR}, {@optfeature Arctic Adept|LLAR:E}, {@optfeature Desert Adept|LLAR:E}, or {@optfeature Jungle Adept|LLAR:E}. Starting at 7th level, you can choose to learn {@optfeature Planar Adept|LLAR:E}.
Knacks learned through this feature do not count against your total Knacks Known, and they can only be replaced by another Knack from the list above.

subclassFeature.name: Mystic Camouflage
entries: You weave illusion magic into your mystical garments. While a creature is wearing a Wasteland Cloak, it can use the Cloak to cast the {@spell invisibility} spell on itself. The spell ends early if the creature is no longer wearing the Cloak.

Each Wasteland Cloak can be used to cast {@spell invisibility} three times, but when its last use is expended, that Cloak loses any beneficial properties. All Wasteland Cloaks regain their uses of {@spell invisibility} when you maintain them during a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Illusory Strikes
entries: You enhance your combat with deadly illusion magic. When you take the Attack action on your turn, you can use a bonus action on that same turn to conjure an Illusory Duplicate of yourself in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet.
This Duplicate makes a single attack with a duplicate of your weapon, dealing force damage on hit. After the attack, hit or miss, the duplicate disappears.

subclassFeature.name: Mirage Transposition
entries: You have mastered the mystical illusory magic of the wilds. You can use Illusory Strikes to conjure a duplicate of yourself and attack, even if you do not take the Attack action.
Moreover, any time you conjure a duplicate of yourself with Illusory Strikes, you can switch places with it after its attack.
Finally, as an action, you can can instantly switch places with a creature wearing a Wasteland Cloak within 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Wrangler
entries: While most Rangers have some skill with animals, those who pursue the life of a Wrangler are true masters at taming wild beasts. Drawing upon their intimate understanding of animal behavior, they tame and guide all sorts of fantastical animals. Rugged and wild, Wranglers are always on the hunt for ever more strange and exotic creatures to tame and befriend.

subclassFeature.name: Beast Tamer
entries: Your intimate understanding of animal behaviors and habits grants you certain benefits. You gain proficiency in {@skill Athletics} or {@skill Animal Handling}. You also gain the following features:

items: You can mark beasts and monstrosities as your {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2||Quarry} without expending a use of Ranger's Quarry.
Whenever you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) check to {@action grapple}, climb, wrestle, or subdue your Quarry, you can add one roll of your Quarry Die to your roll.

Any enchantment spell you have prepared that could normally {@filter only target humanoids|spells|class=alternate ranger (llar)|subclass=alternate ranger: wrangler (llar:e)|school=e|affects creature types=!aberration;!beast;!celestial;!construct;!dragon;!elemental;!fey;!fiend;!giant;humanoid;!monstrosity;!ooze;!plant;!undead} can also be used to target beasts and monstrosities.

subclassFeature.name: Wrangler Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Bring to Heel
entries: You can bend creatures of the wild to your will. Whenever a beast or monstrosity that is {@condition charmed} by you makes a saving throw to end the charmed condition, you can use a reaction to shout at that creature and subtract a roll of your Quarry Die from the result of its saving throw.

You can also command the monsters you tame. Any beast or monstrosity charmed by you acts during your turn for the duration of that charm effect. If you are within 30 feet of the charmed creature and it can hear you, you can use an action to command it to take one of the actions from its stat block.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: Whenever a beast or monstrosity that is charmed by you makes a saving throw to end the charmed condition

subclassFeature.name: Monster Tamer
entries: Your ability to tame and train creatures is near-supernatural in scope. Any Wrangler feature that normally affects beasts and monstrosities also affects celestials, dragons, fey, fiends, giants, plants, and oozes that have an Intelligence score less than or equal to your Ranger level.
Also, you can command any creature that is {@condition charmed} by you to take an action from its stat block with a bonus action, so long as that creature is within 30 feet and can hear you.

subclassFeature.name: Wrangler of Legends
entries: Once you charm a creature, there is little it can do to escape. When you cast an enchantment spell on a beast, monstrosity, or one of the creature types listed for {@subclassFeature Monster Tamer|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|Wrangler|LLAR:E|11}, your {@status concentration} cannot be broken unless you wish it to be.

Moreover, creatures that are {@condition charmed} by you gain all of the benefits of {@classFeature Ranger's Quarry|Alternate Ranger|LLAR|2} (including any of your Knacks that enhance it), and whenever one of these creatures is forced to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to shout and add one roll of your Quarry Die to the result of its roll, so long as it is within 30 feet and can hear you.

foundrySystem.activation.condition: Whenever a creature that is charmed by you is forced to make a saving throw

subclassFeature.name: Elder Haunt
entries: Deep in the world's most elder forests dwell forgotten entities of hunger. You have joined a dark Conclave which draws upon the eldritch power of such entities. Have you sworn to protect the world from this dark hunger? Or, do you serve its desires?

subclassFeature.name: Elder Haunt Form
entries: Unexplainable eldritch horror infuses your magic. When you mark a creature with Ranger's Quarry, you can transform into an Elder Haunt for the duration, gaining these benefits:

entries.name: Gaunt Visage
entries: Your appearance is twisted into a horrifying mockery of yourself. Your Armor Class is equal to 10 + your Wisdom and Constitution modifiers, unless it was higher.

entries.name: Unsettling Presence
entries: Creatures of your choice that begin their turn within 10 feet of you, and can see you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature can only move, use its action, or use bonus action on that turn.

subclassFeature.name: Haunt Spells
entries: You always have certain spells prepared at the Ranger levels in the table below. The spells count as Ranger spells for you, but they don't count against the spells you prepare each day.

subclassFeature.name: Primeval Hunger
entries: The eldritch powers thirst for the blood of the living. While in Elder Haunt form you add your Quarry Die to saving throws, and if you damage your Quarry while you have half of your hit points or fewer remaining, you regain hit points equal to your Quarry damage bonus.
